backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor for the technical support platform, defining data types (Ticket, KBArticle, KBComment, etc.) and state maps for tickets, users, payments, chat, and knowledge base, along with access control, Stripe payment, and HTTP outcall integrations.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report listing two implemented features: removing the chat/ticket history tab from the customer-facing UI, and replacing the full payment requirement with a $1 minimum charge in TicketCreationForm.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-to-two sentence summaries of all frontend source files, serving as a reference for the components, hooks, and styles present in the project.
frontend/index.html: Root HTML entry point for the React frontend, mounting the app into a div with id 'root' and loading the main TypeScript module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Main React app entry using TanStack Router with routes for index, customer login, expert login, knowledge base, and payment success pages, wrapped in QueryClientProvider and ThemeProvider with lazy-loaded page components.
frontend/src/components/AdminLoginTracki: Admin dashboard component that displays a table of user login events fetched from the backend and provides a CSV export button with download functionality.
frontend/src/components/AgentPerformance: Renders a card with a table listing technician agents, their online/offline status, and estimated tickets handled and average resolution time based on resolved ticket counts.
AI chat assistant component with a local IT knowledge base that matches user queries to predefined troubleshooting guides for topics like printers, BSOD, slow computers, passwords, and Wi-Fi, with a handover option to a live technician.
frontend/src/components/AnalyticsDashboa: Displays a full analytics dashboard with metric cards (total, open, resolved tickets, resolution rate), ticket volume and priority charts, and the agent performance table, fetching data via custom hooks.
frontend/src/components/ChatFeedbackDial: A modal dialog that lets users rate their support session on a 1–10 slider with an optional comment, showing a thank-you confirmation on successful submission.
frontend/src/components/ChatSection.tsx: Chat interface for a support ticket that displays messages, handles sending new messages, marks messages as read, and includes a technician-facing payment toggle that opens a payment request modal.
frontend/src/components/ClientPortal.tsx: Customer-facing portal with tabs for viewing existing support tickets and browsing support plans (Basic and Premium), plus a dialog for creating new tickets.
frontend/src/components/CustomerDashboar: Top-level customer dashboard with tabs for the client portal, chat, knowledge base, and payment sections, plus a background controller that auto-shows a payment modal when a technician enables the payment toggle.
frontend/src/components/CustomerLogin.ts: Customer login page using Internet Identity authentication with a two-column layout featuring branding imagery and a login card; redirects to home on successful authentication.
Expert dashboard with tabbed navigation covering chat, ticket list, analytics, knowledge base, admin login tracking, and a resolved-ticket history timeline view.
frontend/src/components/ExpertLogin.tsx: Expert portal login page using Internet Identity with a violet-themed two-column layout; redirects to home after successful authentication.
frontend/src/components/ExpertProfileCar: Displays an expert's avatar, display name, role, and online/offline status badge in a card component.
frontend/src/components/FloatingAIChatBu: Floating button fixed to the bottom-left that toggles an AI assistant chat panel overlay with a pulsing animation.
frontend/src/components/FloatingChatButt: Floating chat button fixed to the bottom-right that shows a proactive chat invitation after 8 seconds and navigates to the home/chat route on acceptance.
frontend/src/components/Footer.tsx: Site footer displaying a support email address and a 'Built with caffeine.ai' attribution with the current year.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: Sticky app header with the PKG Tech Support logo, knowledge base navigation, dark/light theme toggle, and an Internet Identity login/logout button.
Displays a full knowledge base article with metadata (views, date, tags, category) and increments the view count on mount. Admins can edit or delete the article via action buttons.
Form component for creating or editing a knowledge base article, with fields for title, category, body, and tags. Calls create or update mutation hooks depending on whether an existing article is provided.
frontend/src/components/KnowledgeBaseVie: Main knowledge base page that supports browsing all articles, searching, and filtering by category, with navigation to article detail and editor views. Admin users can create and edit articles.
frontend/src/components/LoginPrompt.tsx: Marketing landing page shown to unauthenticated users, featuring a banner, device icons, service skills, pricing tiers, testimonials, and a portfolio section. Includes a call-to-action that navigates to login.
frontend/src/components/MainContent.tsx: Renders either the CustomerDashboard or ExpertDashboard based on whether the user profile indicates a technician role, with a welcome header.
frontend/src/components/MetricCard.tsx: Reusable card component that displays a labeled metric value with an optional subtitle, icon, and trend indicator (up/down/neutral).
frontend/src/components/OfflineContactFo: Contact form displayed when all experts are offline, allowing users to submit their name, email, and message. Shows a success confirmation after simulated submission.
frontend/src/components/OpenTicketsByPri: Renders a bar chart displaying open support tickets distributed across Low, Medium, High, and Critical priority levels, with counts derived proportionally from the total open ticket count.
frontend/src/components/PaymentRequestMo: A modal dialog that presents a payment request to the user for a support session, supporting UPI/QR and card payment methods with a post-payment confirmation state.
frontend/src/components/PaymentSection.t: Displays a Stripe-powered payment card for purchasing technical support, showing a loading state, a disabled placeholder when Stripe is not configured, or an active checkout button when configured.
frontend/src/components/PreChatForm.tsx: A modal form that collects a user's name, email, and issue description before starting a support chat, with inline validation and submission handling.
frontend/src/components/ProactiveChatInv: A small animated invitation widget that prompts the user to start a chat with a support expert, offering accept and dismiss actions.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetupModa: A modal that collects a display name from a new user and saves their caller profile via a mutation hook to complete onboarding.
frontend/src/components/ServiceHistoryTi: Displays a chronological timeline of a customer's resolved support tickets, with loading skeletons, error state, and an empty state when no resolved tickets exist.
frontend/src/components/SLAIndicator.tsx: Displays a live-updating SLA countdown badge or banner showing time remaining until a ticket's due timestamp, with visual states for breached and warning conditions.
frontend/src/components/TicketCreationFo: Multi-step form for creating a support ticket: technician selection, Stripe $1 payment checkout, and ticket creation after payment confirmation.
frontend/src/components/TicketDetailView: Renders detailed information for a single support ticket, including status badge, customer/technician info, timestamps, and a status update control for technicians.
frontend/src/components/TicketListView.t: Lists support tickets with status filtering, supporting both user-specific and admin (all-tickets) views, and navigates to a detail view on ticket selection.
frontend/src/components/TicketVolumeChar: Renders a line chart showing daily ticket volume over the last 30 days using mock-generated data derived from a total ticket count.
Simple animated three-dot typing indicator component used to signal that someone is composing a message.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor using the current Internet Identity, invalidating dependent queries whenever the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAnalytics.ts: Custom hook that fetches analytics metrics (total, open, resolved tickets, resolution rate) from the backend actor, refreshing every 30 seconds.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication, managing login/logout state, identity persistence, and AuthClient creation with configurable options.
frontend/src/hooks/useKnowledgeBase.ts: Exports React Query hooks for CRUD operations on knowledge base articles, including fetching, searching, filtering by category, creating, updating, deleting, and incrementing view counts.
frontend/src/hooks/usePaymentToggle.ts: Provides React Query hooks to get and set payment toggle state (including persistent state) for support tickets, with polling every 5 seconds.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Exports a collection of React Query hooks covering user profiles, admin checks, technician availability, and chat message operations against the backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useTickets.ts: Provides React Query hooks for fetching user and admin support tickets, retrieving a single ticket, updating ticket status, and creating new support tickets.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports Google Fonts, sets up Tailwind CSS, and defines CSS custom properties for a full light/dark theme including colors, typography, and component-level design tokens.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, storing, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence support.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with custom fonts, CSS-variable-based colors, border radii, box shadows, and animations including accordion and pulse-ring keyframes.
project_state.json: Project state tracking file recording existing features, pending action requests, and architectural notes for a customer/expert panel project focused on removing chat history, disabling auto-scroll, and simplifying ticket payment to a $1 minimum charge.
